## Jan 28, 2026 | [Team DevOps](https://www.google.com/calendar/event?eid=MXRkOXU0amIzdG5iZXVzN2c2Zmh0NTNqc3RfMjAyNjAxMDdUMTUwMDAwWiBkcmV3LnJvc2VidXNoQHJ1cmFsaW5ub3ZhdGlvbi51cw)

Dataverse ideation

- [Harvard dataverse](https://dataverse.harvard.edu/)  
- [Urban institution charitable dataverse](https://nccsgit.urban.org/nccs/)  
- [Geocorr](https://mcdc.missouri.edu/applications/)  
- [NFI Datamart](https://apps.fs.usda.gov/fia/datamart/datamart.html)  
- [Tidycensus](https://walker-data.com/tidycensus/articles/basic-usage.html)

Features and general descriptions:

- Data download  
- R packages  
  - Example code (beyond vignettes)  
- Project (report/applications) links tagged by resources used  
- Ontology of Rural socio-economic metrics used in our work  
- Nicely designed/easy to use  
- Externally facing   
- Geographic crosswalks (with citations)  
- Rural definitions  
- Website is procedurally generated using a config file (e.g. yml)  
  - Agent can read without scraping the website

- Github link to dataset prep  
- Blogposts linked at the bottom that use that dataset  
- Tags of dataset topics  
- Including geographical transformation processes, like the crosswalk example  
- A visual map of how datasets are related to each other or to government sources  
- Journal/Blog highlight features with descriptions, URL dl links?  
- User-friendly interface  
- R package/code github link to validate code; further information on data mgmt/xformation  
- Data viz repository (cori-branded) with general (but important\!) figures; line chart estab entry rate; self employment; patents, population swings, net migration, ec.  
  - Viz as teach about data  
  - Viz as download for use and distribute CORI brand

Synthesized features:

* Project works cited  
  * How far back do we want to go with linking projects?  
* Public facing R packages (Github) accessible  
  * Our current publicly facing packages  
    * BEA  
    * FCC  
    * Rural Definitions  
    * Cori.charts  
    * dform  
    * Soon: BDS, COG, QCEW,  patents (USPTO)  
* Data sharing  
  * Source location S3  
* Research based ontology (what we care about)  
* Change log  
* References to team based blogs  
  * What does it look like to bring the blog  
    * Migrate the blog to rural dataverse  
* How we do our work  
  * Techniques we use in our work   
  * *“Stats Lab” for Rural*  
* Config file that cori (the cori.agent) can read  
* Data/data viz catalog

[Our current example](https://erc.ruralinnovation.us/)

Example quarto site that   
[Github link](https://github.com/UrbanInstitute/nccs): 

- Review layout as a basis for the individual pages

![][image1]

Website schema

- Source/base elements:  
  - Data sources  
  - R packages  
  - S3 buckets  
  - Journals/articles/citations used in our works

- Product facing: products are built using the base elements (tagging base elements)  
  - Applications  
  - Project pages  
  - Blog posts (social media posts)  
  - Articles
